articles.title: OpenAI Announces GPT-5 with Advanced Reasoning Capabilities
articles.description: OpenAI has unveiled GPT-5, its most capable model yet, featuring significantly improved reasoning, multimodal understanding, and a new architecture designed for complex multi-step problem solving across domains.

articles.title: EU AI Act Enforcement Begins: What Organizations Need to Know
articles.description: The European Union's comprehensive AI Act enters its first enforcement phase this week, requiring high-risk AI systems to meet transparency and safety standards. Companies worldwide are scrambling to ensure compliance.

articles.title: Stanford Study: AI Tutors Improve Student Outcomes by 30% in Underserved Schools
articles.description: A large-scale study from Stanford HAI finds that AI-powered tutoring systems significantly boosted math and reading scores among students in low-income districts, though researchers caution about equitable access to the technology.

articles.title: Google DeepMind's AlphaFold 3 Maps New Drug Targets for Rare Diseases
articles.description: The latest version of AlphaFold has identified hundreds of previously unknown protein structures linked to rare genetic diseases, accelerating potential drug discovery timelines from years to months.

articles.title: Anthropic Raises $5B Series D as Enterprise AI Demand Surges
articles.description: Claude-maker Anthropic has closed a $5 billion funding round, valuing the AI safety company at over $60 billion. The funding will support scaling safety research and enterprise product development.

articles.title: AI Bias in Healthcare: New Study Reveals Disparities in Diagnostic Tools
articles.description: Researchers at MIT found that widely-used AI diagnostic systems show significant accuracy gaps across racial and ethnic groups, renewing calls for mandatory bias auditing in medical AI applications.

articles.title: China Launches National AI Education Initiative for K-12 Schools
articles.description: China's Ministry of Education announced a nationwide program to integrate AI literacy into primary and secondary school curricula, aiming to train 10 million students in foundational AI skills by 2028.

articles.title: Microsoft Copilot Gets Social Work-Specific Templates for Documentation
articles.description: Microsoft has partnered with NASW to develop AI-powered templates within Copilot specifically designed for social work case documentation, progress notes, and treatment planning while maintaining client confidentiality.

articles.title: UK Government Proposes AI Watermarking Requirement for All Generated Content
articles.description: The UK's Department for Science has proposed mandatory digital watermarks on all AI-generated text, images, and video, aiming to combat deepfakes and misinformation ahead of upcoming elections.

articles.title: New Open-Source AI Model Rivals GPT-4 at Fraction of the Cost
articles.description: A coalition of academic institutions has released an open-source language model that benchmarks competitively with proprietary systems, potentially democratizing access to advanced AI for researchers and educators.

articles.title: How Professors Are Using AI to Create More Inclusive Assessments
articles.description: A growing number of higher education institutions are leveraging AI tools to design assessments that accommodate diverse learning styles and reduce cultural bias in testing, according to a new EdSurge report.

articles.title: Japan Develops AI-Powered Elder Care Companion Robots
articles.description: Japanese tech firms have unveiled a new generation of AI companion robots designed for elderly care facilities, capable of emotional recognition, medication reminders, and real-time health monitoring.

articles.title: AI Ethics Board Calls for Moratorium on Emotion Recognition in Hiring
articles.description: A coalition of AI ethics researchers and civil rights organizations is urging governments to ban the use of AI emotion recognition systems in hiring processes, citing unreliable science and discriminatory outcomes.

articles.title: Google Launches Free AI Literacy Course for University Faculty
articles.description: Google has released a comprehensive, free online course designed to help university professors understand, evaluate, and thoughtfully integrate generative AI tools into their teaching practices across disciplines.

articles.title: India Announces $2B Investment in Domestic AI Infrastructure
articles.description: India's government has committed $2 billion toward building sovereign AI compute infrastructure, including data centers and training facilities, as part of its strategy to become a global AI development hub.

articles.title: Researchers Develop AI System That Detects Social Worker Burnout Early
articles.description: A team at Columbia University has created an AI tool that analyzes workplace patterns to identify early signs of burnout among social workers, potentially reducing turnover in an already understaffed profession.
